mission.title: opentelemetry: New meeting recording workflow
mission.description:
Resolves #1406
Resolves #964
Resolves #935
Resolves #863
Resolves #715
Resolves #451

New meeting workflow is to no longer upload meetings to YouTube, but instead to make Zoom cloud recordings available via Google spreadsheet using Zapier integration:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/218610/231894712-fd46d01c-0661-49dd-984c-44f9b5201b8f.png)

Here's an example recording: https://zoom.us/rec/share/6VEDYb5zV-hDAT-mDr5W_A9FDYrE6WyTeWMRWz8xqKgJKOypdEwr9rDDerW2xMDj.zz6qA84Yxqces8s4

Note that this includes Zoom chat messages which YouTube videos were previously missing.

The naming relies on splitting out separate Zoom ID per meeting series, which is documented in #1415.

I verified that all Zoom cloud recordings exist back to the beginning of the project, so expiring too soon doesn't appear to be a problem, though we may still wish to expire them (deferring this issue to #562).

Note that this Google spreadsheet only contains meeting recordings starting from 4/10/2023 (w

resolution.summary:
The old YouTube link send people to a page with no recordings, and the entire archive appears to be gone.

This doc update reflects proposal here: https://github.com/open-telemetry/community/pull/1431/
steps: Review the issue discussion for context
Apply the fix from the linked pull request
